title: The Best AI Web Scraper in 2026? I Tested 3
transcript: Thunderbit, Brightdata, and ScrapingBee. These are three of the most talked about web scrapers in 2026. I constantly use web scraping and wanted to see which one was best, so I gave all three the same job. Scrape 200 products and every review of a real e-commerce site and end up with a clean structured JSON. And one of them did it better and faster than the others by far. Can you guess which one? Web scraping in 2026 is different. There are captchas everywhere. They are my archnemesis. Single-page apps, bot fingerprinting, the right tool depends on the shape of the task. So, I gave them all the same task. Find a real e-commerce site, grab 200 products, name plus price plus image plus reviews plus star rating plus subpage spec data, and just give it back to me really clean. Whatever wins wins. And every tool gets the same setup, the same screen time, the same metrics, and I will let the numbers tell the story. Also, full disclosure, Thunderbit is sponsoring this video. They are also one of the three tools being tested, but their team noted to me that I need to keep this comparison objective and let the demo speak for itself. So, that is exactly what we are doing. If you didn't know, Thunderbit is an AI-powered web scraper with two surfaces. You've got a Chrome extension for two-click extraction and an API for programmatic use. The pitch is pretty simple. It is the same thing as hiring an intern. It reads websites, it writes down the table for you, and you just describe what it is you want, and the AI figures out where it is on the page. And so, one API call gives you clean structured data. You send a URL and a JSON schema, and you get back clean JSON. There are no CSS selectors, no XPath, no per site config, thankfully. And when a site redesigns, it does not break. The AI reads semantic meaning, thankfully, not brittle selectors as per usual. So, let's start the test with Bright Data. Then drill into each product detail page and extract the full spec table. That is subpage scraping with zero extra setup. I wanted this for so long. An AI that knew how I did it because I described it in the schema. All right, so the final tally, three columns, Bright Data, ScrapingBee, and Thunderbit. Metric counters, same tasks, three tools, lines of code, time lapse, captures hit, output quality, I will let the numbers speak for themselves. All three tools work. The question is which fits your stack. Bright Data brings industrial scale proxy infrastructure and that's its strength.
